grimes~artangels: The title track, “Artangels”, or “Art Angels”, depending on what you prefer on a given day.
pro8l3m~skrable: Pierwszy singiel promujący nowe wydawnictwo PRO8L3M pt.
rammstein~stripped: This song is a cover of Depeche Mode – Stripped, which was released in 1986 as part of Depeche Mode’s album Black Celebration.
be-lakor~abeyance: It depicts an old man who has ventured to a distant place to visit some sort of elder or shaman. He has done so in the hope of ingesting a potion of sorts.
in-flames~only-for-the-weak: “Only For The Weak” is one of In Flames' most played songs live.
thank-you-scientist~fxmldr: The first single from the 3rd studio album ‘Terraformer’ by New Jersey-based prog rock band Thank You Scientist, released on April 12, 2019.
the-dillinger-escape-plan~the-threat-pos: Nuclear Weapons are devastating W.M.D.’s and they are capable of destroying entire cities, or even entire states!
david-bowie~heroes: It’s believed the track was inspired by German band Neu! whom Bowie was a fan of at the time.
metallica~for-whom-the-bell-tolls: Metallica’s take on Hemingway’s novel of the same name about the reproach of modern warfare and the combatants' impending demise during the Spanish Civil War. This continues their string of literary adaptations that runs from the Bible through Dalton Trumbo.
asking-alexandria~under-denver: “Under Denver” is basically an alternative rock song and it talks about the Denver’s airport conspiracies, as Danny Worsnop, the songwriter, said to Alternative Press: OK, this was a very fun song. Usually, a song takes me maybe an hour to write.
asking-alexandria~hopelessly-hopeful: He said this about the writing process: I’ve performed open-heart surgery on myself. It’s about going through the trauma that is exploring one’s inner psyche, bettering myself, and fixing whatever the hell is wrong with me.
crystal-castles~magic-spells: “Magic Spells” is one of Crystal Castles' more relaxed songs.
korn~die-yet-another-night: This is one of three songs of the upcoming album “The Serenity of Suffering”, due October 21st 2016, which was played exclusively on SiriusXM Octane radio on October 19th, 2016.
in-flames~ropes: Anders Friden said in an interview with Songfacts: ‘This song is about helping someone, being there for someone, and how important that is when you see your friends or your family go down. How that is hurting you, but at the same time a lot of people just don’t want to take part in that and reach out with a helping hand, because it would be too tough on them.
in-flames~where-the-dead-ships-dwell: The title metaphorically depicts the profound isolation and desolation felt by individuals who perceive themselves as disconnected from the world, akin to being aboard a lifeless vessel, enhancing the sense of hopelessness.
woods-of-ypres~keeper-of-the-ledger: Track 3 on the album Woods 5: Grey Skies & Electric Light personifies nature, being the force that gives and takes all life on Earth.
rammstein~heirate-mich: A deeply disturbing song, ‘Heirate Mich’ means ‘Marry Me’.
rush~red-barchetta: “Red Barchetta” is the story of a car chase in an Orwellian future. The song’s narrator seeks to escape urban dystopia by driving an illegal antique motor car around the countryside, while two hovering law enforcement vehicles pursue and attempt to apprehend him.
a-day-to-remember~all-signs-point-to-lau: “All Signs Point to Lauderdale” is about someone who doesn’t fit in with the people who live in his town. He almost feels like he’s trapped, and all he desires is to find somewhere where he can belong.
tv-on-the-radio~wolf-like-me: Tunde tells the story of becoming a werewolf as an allegory for the uncontrollable desire that he has for the recipient of the song.
the-cranberries~zombie: The Cranberries describe the violence in “The Troubles,” the decades-long conflict in Northern Ireland between nationalists (mainly self-identified as Irish or Roman Catholic) and unionists (mainly self-identified as British or Protestant).
trivium~caustic-are-the-ties-that-bind: The song details the social bonds which end up serving as chains to our progression in life.
led-zeppelin~immigrant-song: The lyrics, as explained by Robert Plant and documented by Wikipedia, are influenced by the band visiting Iceland, a land first settled by Vikings: We weren’t being pompous (about the song)… We did come from the land of the ice and snow.
boston~more-than-a-feeling: This is the first track off of Boston’s self-titled debut album, which describes the emotions that the singer is going through when he hears a particular song that takes him back to his memories of Marianne.
linkin-park~until-it-s-gone: The seventh track on Linkin Park’s sixth studio album, The Hunting Party, “Until It’s Gone” builds on the famous proverb: “You don’t know what you have until its gone”.
bloodhound-gang~three-point-one-four: The tittle of the song is “Three Point One Four” which can be transcripted to “3.14”, the pi number (π).
papa-roach~scars: During live performances before playing the song Shaddix often talks about how the song saved his life.
machine-head~is-there-anybody-out-there-: When Rob wrote this song (before the Dimebash controversy happened), he wanted it to sound as angry as he possibly could.
dream-theater~voices: Written by guitarist John Petrucci, “Voices” is a 10-minute progressive piece that talks about schizophrenia via various religious imagery. It is the second and longest part of the “A Mind Beside Itself” suite, the other two parts being Erotomania and The Silent Man.
dream-theater~prophets-of-war: Written by vocalist James LaBrie, “Prophets of War” talks about causes and conseguences of war, probably referring to US attacking Iraq.
rammstein~eifersucht: “Eifersucht” is about jealousy in its most macabre forms. In that way, it perfectly and humorously depicts the sometimes morbid thoughts that occur when we are jealous of each other.
pearl-jam~brain-of-j: Choosing to tie in an event that birthed hundreds of conspiracy theories (via the title and lyrics) sets an ominous tone as an album opener.
deftones~hearts-wires: Deftones 5th track of their album “Gore” entitled “Hearts/Wires” is a true testament not only to their listeners through the various epitomes of emotion they are known to display in previous albums but of Chino Moreno’s visceral and impacting lyricism through songwriting.
ted-nugent~stranglehold: “Stranglehold” is a single and the first track from Ted Nugent’s self-titled 1975 album.
serj-tankian~deserving: This song describes the self-doubts that arise during a relationship. The protagonist wonders if he’s good enough for his partner.
zz-top~sharp-dressed-man: “Sharp Dressed Man” is one of ZZ Top’s best known songs, performed by ZZ Top from their signature album Eliminator released in 1983.
deadmau5~edit-your-friends: Edit Your Friends is the 12th song from the album Edit Your Friends.
nightwish~i-want-my-tears-back: “I Want My Tears Back”, the fifth song in the 2011 Nightwish album Imaginaerum, continues the melancholy prevalent in the other songs with a strikingly upbeat, danceable rhythm.
stone-temple-pilots~plush: “Plush” was the second single from the Stone Temple Pilots‘ debut album Core.
marilyn-manson~odds-of-even: “Odds of Even” is the final track on The Pale Emperor; Manson explains his true intentions, and proves that he’s not the “Antichrist Superstar” that the world has thought him to be for the past 2 decades.
massive-attack~protection: The title track from the band’s second album, “Protection” is a collaboration between Massive Attack and Tracey Thorn, the lead singer of British duo Everything But the Girl.
tv-on-the-radio~blind: Track #3 on TV on the Radio’s first major release EP “Young Liars”.
the-police~every-breath-you-take: “Every Breath You Take” is, to this day, still perceived to be a love song.
tool~third-eye: The narrator alludes to being on the borderline of a spiritual awakening, and uses some kind of psychedelic to “pry open” his third eye in order to reconnect with himself. If taken as a direct narrative of MJK’s life, it could be seen as a story of Maynard using psychedelics to confront his traumatic childhood and become whole.
trivium~shogun: Shogun entails the thoughts of a Samurai about the Shogunate era code of honour, Bushidō , as he establishes his morality , fights a battle to the death and criticises another Samurai who has broken the Bushidō code of honour.

slayer~epidemic: The eighth song from Reign In Blood album.
alice-in-chains~choke: The song explores the strain and inevitability of a fractured relationship, where unresolved tension and pride create a suffocating atmosphere. It conveys the difficulty of staying connected when emotional walls and past wounds dominate, emphasizing personal responsibility for one’s choices.
ozzy-osbourne~latimer-s-mercy: The song was inspired by a real-life farmer who killed his suffering daughter in 1993. At a press conference launching Scream in Canada, Osbourne explained that the song chronicles Saskatchewan farmer Robert Latimer’s decision to end his disabled daughter’s life.

megadeth~killing-is-my-business-and-busi: This song is about a hitman who has been hired to kill someone. After he gets paid, however, it turns out that he has also been assigned to eliminate the guy who gave him the first contract.
megadeth~this-was-my-life: This song is about Mustaine having wanted to kill someone he had an affair with, but didn’t because he knew he’d then go to jail. This is a confession of the madness he felt at the time.
mot-rhead~killed-by-death: In this song, Lemmy says he’s not easy to get along with, until death comes for him.

mot-rhead~too-late-too-late: It talks about a man leaving his lover because she acts horribly like a “bitch” as the song puts it.
deftones~beware: Serving as a warning and a thematic vehicle to discuss addiction, the crux of Beware lies within two things: drugs and other destructive behaviours.

fear-factory~dog-day-sunrise: This track is a Head of David cover, off the Dustbowl album.
guns-n-roses~you-re-crazy: Axl berates a girl who’s out of her mind and/or excessively horny, that wants to have sex all the time.

he-is-legend~the-seduction: The narrator appears to be the protagonist of the story, seeking to find “Juliet” even though she poisoned him at the beginning of the song.

placebo~song-to-say-goodbye: The song deals with the themes of addiction, and its influence upon the relationships of those involved. It builds upon its subject matter from wanting to be rid of something you’re emotionally tied to.

static-dress~sweet: is the first release from Static Dress in 2021, it was teased over the bands social media.

primus~jerry-was-a-race-car-driver: “Jerry Was a Race Car Driver” is a song about a young man named Jerry who loves driving his fast car around his hometown of El Sobrante. It also compares Jerry’s frivolous lifestyle with the exploits of a fireman named Captain Pierce.

linkin-park~p5hng-me-a-wy-mike-shinoda-r: The official remix of Pushing Me Away featuring Stephen Richards from Taproot.

pro8l3m~777moneymaker: Parę słów od producenta:Ten bit ma dosyć wyjątkowy aranż i chyba to się nam w nim właśnie najbardziej podobało.

dream-theater~scarred: The tenth track on Dream Theater’s 1994 album Awake.
tv-on-the-radio~ambulance: Track #5 from TV On the Radio’s 2004 debut album, ‘Desperate Youth, Blood Thirsty Babes’.
thank-you-scientist~terraformer: The second single from the 3rd studio album by New Jersey-based prog rock band Thank You Scientist, released on May 10, 2019.
the-dillinger-escape-plan~when-i-lost-my: Second track found on the 2013 album One of Us is the Killer, the lyrics deal with a lifestyle based on leeching off of other people’s fame and success, in an effort to attain these things for yourself.
